question: Q7
title: PM2.5 vs Heat Stress (WBGT) Relationship Analysis
research_question: What is the relationship between PM2.5 and heat indicators, controlling for meteorological and temporal factors? Is there heterogeneity across sites?

regression.interpretation: Each 1F increase in WBGT associates with 0.399 ug/m3 increase in PM2.5

key_findings: Moderate positive relationship: r=0.3598 (p<0.001), explaining 12.9% of PM2.5 variance
Each 1F WBGT increase associates with 0.399 ug/m3 PM2.5 increase
Significant site heterogeneity: correlations range 0.229 to 0.620 (2.7-fold variation)
Berkeley Community Garden has strongest relationship (r=0.62, R2=38.4%)
Mary Soo Hoo Park has weakest (r=0.229)
Higher heat stress periods coincide with elevated PM2.5, creating compound health risks
76.1% of observations exceed WHO PM2.5 guideline (5 ug/m3)
